label: Settings API
title: Settings API
lead: Register native settings rows in the vanilla options menu with CUCoreLib handling save, reset, apply, and labels.
bodyHtml:
Register settings in startup

Use ModOptionsRegistry.Register from your plugin startup to add rows to the normal game options menu. CUCoreLib appends real vanilla Setting objects, so the game keeps owning rendering, settings.json, immediate apply, menu close saves, and Reset to Default.

using CUCoreLib.Data;
using CUCoreLib.Registries;
using UnityEngine;

private void Awake()
{
    ModOptionsRegistry.Register(ModOptionDefinition.Bool(
        "glassworks.game.enabled",
        "Enable Glassworks",
        "Controls whether Glassworks content is active.",
        Setting.SettingCategory.Game,
        true,
        value => PlayerPrefs.SetInt("Glassworks_Enabled", value ? 1 : 0)
    ));
}

Custom categories

Pass a plain string category when you want your mod to get its own tab button on the right side of the settings menu. CUCoreLib will reuse the same custom tab for matching strings, and long custom pages can be scrolled with the mouse wheel.

ModOptionsRegistry.Register(ModOptionDefinition.Bool(
    "glassworks.furnace.enabled",
    "Enable kiln sparks",
    "Controls extra kiln ambience and helpers.",
    "Glassworks",
    true,
    value => PlayerPrefs.SetInt("Glassworks_KilnSparks", value ? 1 : 0)
));

Supported row types

BuilderVanilla rowStored value
ModOptionDefinition.FloatSlider with optional formatterfloat
ModOptionDefinition.IntInteger inputint
ModOptionDefinition.BoolTogglebool
ModOptionDefinition.DropdownDropdownSelected choice index
ModOptionDefinition.KeybindKeybind buttonKeyCode

IDs must be namespaced, like glassworks.audio.clinkvolume. Labels, descriptions, and dropdown labels are registered as literal locale text for the vanilla gameset{id} keys. The category argument may be either a vanilla Setting.SettingCategory enum or a custom string.

Apply callbacks

The callback receives the current value after the player changes the control or after settings are loaded. Keep it small: write your own persistent mirror if needed, update runtime state, and let vanilla settings handle the main save file.

ModOptionsRegistry.Register(ModOptionDefinition.Float(
    "glassworks.audio.clinkvolume",
    "Glass clink volume",
    "Scales Glassworks custom sound effects.",
    Setting.SettingCategory.Audio,
    PlayerPrefs.GetFloat("Glassworks_ClinkVolume", 0.8f),
    0f,
    1f,
    value =>
    {
        PlayerPrefs.SetFloat("Glassworks_ClinkVolume", value);
        PlayerPrefs.Save();
    },
    value => Mathf.RoundToInt(value * 100f) + "%"
));

Validation rules

  • Each ID must contain a namespace dot and must be unique.
  • Float and int ranges must have min <= max.
  • Dropdowns must have at least one choice, no duplicate choice keys, and a default index inside the choice list.
  • Only vanilla row types are supported. Custom prefab-backed rows should stay mod-local.
bodyText: Register settings in startup Use ModOptionsRegistry.Register from your plugin startup to add rows to the normal game options menu. CUCoreLib appends real vanilla Setting objects, so the game keeps owning rendering, settings.json , immediate apply, menu close saves, and Reset to Default. using CUCoreLib.Data; using CUCoreLib.Registries; using UnityEngine; private void Awake() { ModOptionsRegistry.Register(ModOptionDefinition.Bool( "glassworks.game.enabled", "Enable Glassworks", "Controls whether Glassworks content is active.", Setting.SettingCategory.Game, true, value => PlayerPrefs.SetInt("Glassworks_Enabled", value ? 1 : 0) )); } Custom categories Pass a plain string category when you want your mod to get its own tab button on the right side of the settings menu. CUCoreLib will reuse the same custom tab for matching strings, and long custom pages can be scrolled with the mouse wheel. ModOptionsRegistry.Register(ModOptionDefinition.Bool( "glassworks.furnace.enabled", "Enable kiln sparks", "Controls extra kiln ambience and helpers.", "Glassworks", true, value => PlayerPrefs.SetInt("Glassworks_KilnSparks", value ? 1 : 0) )); Supported row types Builder Vanilla row Stored value ModOptionDefinition.Float Slider with optional formatter float ModOptionDefinition.Int Integer input int ModOptionDefinition.Bool Toggle bool ModOptionDefinition.Dropdown Dropdown Selected choice index ModOptionDefinition.Keybind Keybind button KeyCode IDs must be namespaced, like glassworks.audio.clinkvolume . Labels, descriptions, and dropdown labels are registered as literal locale text for the vanilla gameset{id} keys. The category argument may be either a vanilla Setting.SettingCategory enum or a custom string. Apply callbacks The callback receives the current value after the player changes the control or after settings are loaded. Keep it small: write your own persistent mirror if needed, update runtime state, and let vanilla settings handle the main save file. Custom prefab-backed rows should stay mod-local.
